description_long: This object is a classic black rotary dial corded telephone, featuring a robust rectangular base with a glossy finish. The handset is thick and ergonomically curved, resting on a cradle at the top of the base, and is connected to the main unit by a coiled cord. The rotary dial is prominently positioned on the slanted front face, with numbers 1 through 9 and 0 arranged in a circular pattern around a metallic finger stop. The base includes a small rectangular button or indicator below the dial, likely for line control or ringer adjustment. The handset cradle is integrated into the top of the base, with visible mechanical hookswitch components. The overall design is utilitarian and durable, typical of mid-20th-century telephones found in homes and offices.
description: A glossy black rotary dial corded telephone with a coiled cord, ergonomic handset, and a prominent circular dial on a sturdy rectangular base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, number markings, finger stop, and the cradle holding the handset.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the handset and the slanted dial face, with the coiled cord visible at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the handset, the cradle, and the coiled cord extending from the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handset profile, cradle, and the side of the rotary dial.
